figures.caption: Figure 1. Schematic of the configuration. An upstream airfoil generates a vortex through a prescribed heaving and pitching motion, which subsequently interacts with a downstream airfoil free to heave in the transverse flow direction.

Figure 2. Flowchart of the tightly coupled fluid–structure interaction (FSI) algorithm. At each timestep, the fluid and structural solvers iterate until the lift force converges before advancing the simulation.

Figure 3. Meshing strategy for the two airfoil domain includes structured body-fitted mesh layers on the airfoils and a structured far-field mesh connected with an unstructured mesh between the airfoils where maximum relative mesh motion occurs.
Figure 4. Spanwise vorticity contours showing the vortex gust and freely-flying airfoil prior to impingement. The dash-dot lines correspond to vortex trajectories.
Figure 5. Airfoil’s heave and lift coefficient in response to vortex interactions. Dashed lines correspond to approximate vortex impingement time.
Figure 6. Heave motion contributions to 𝐶𝐿 for CW2.5. Dashed lines correspond to approximate impingement time.
Figure 7. Heave motion contributions to𝐶𝐿 for CCW2.5. Dashed lines correspond to approximate impingement time.

Figure 8. Spanwise-vorticity contours (top) and pressure contours (bottom) in the region around the airfoil after vortex impingement for CW2.5 and CW2.5-S. In the pressure panels, contour lines of the 𝑄-criterion are overlaid to identify vortical structures.

Figure 9. Spanwise-vorticity contours (top) and pressure contours (bottom) in the region around the airfoil after vortex impingement for CCW2.5 and CCW2.5-S. In the pressure panels, contour lines of the 𝑄-criterion are overlaid to identify vortical structures.
Figure 10. Comparison between 𝐶𝐿 and 𝐶𝐿 for various direct impingement cases. Dashed lines correspond to approximate impingement time.
Figure 11. Spanwise vorticity contour of the region around the airfoil in the post-impingement stage for CW0, CCW0, CW5, and CCW5.
Figure 12. Spanwise vorticity contour of the region around the airfoil in the post-impingement stage for CW2.5a, CW2.5b, CCW2.5a, and CCW2.5b.
Figure 13. Predicted vs. actual heave trajectory for cases at different density ratio. Dashed lines correspond to instance of vortex impingement.
Table 3. Kinematic parameters in Eqns. A 2 and A 3 for all vortex–airfoil configurations. Angles are given in degrees.
Figure 14. Schematic of the FSI validation demonstrating an elastically mounted heaving circular cylinder in incoming flow.
